transcript.text: How was the holidays?
Did y'all travel anywhere?
Just went back to Boston for a little bit, and then it's like a cesspool over there, so I ended up getting sick, which like everyone is, I feel like, but how about you?
Oh man, that's rough.
Are you feeling any better?
Yeah, no, I'm fine.
Go figure.
Nice.
Yeah, I mean...

I'm in Houston, and my fiance, her family, they're originally from Monterey, Mexico, so we actually just got back from there a couple days ago, and it's underrated, you know, like everybody goes to Mexico City, but Monterey, it's like in the mountains, it's really cool, kind of reminded me of like Medellin, Colombia, I don't know if y'all have been anywhere in Latin America.
I've been to Mexico City, how far is it from Mexico City?
Probably like a two-hour flight or something like that, maybe like a five-hour drive, yeah.
Oh, wow.
Yeah, I've only been there, and then I've been to Calvo, given that's just closer.
Both me and Andy are in LA, so very easy flight from here.
Awesome, awesome.
Yeah, I've been to Mexico City as well, but I'm definitely going to vouch for Monterrey now, but I know we've got like 28 minutes.
Do y have a hard stop?
Do we have a little bit of wiggle room?
Yeah, potentially I have a little bit of wiggle room.
I think I have a break in between calls, so it should be good.
But I can give an intro on kind of reasoning for the call, and then would love to just really just learn a little bit more.
Really, the premise of this call is, you know, we are the Ventures team here, so we kind of have the tip of the spear when it comes to innovation.
So we work directly with our business units.
We have two funds.
We have a strategic fund and a financial fund.

But like obviously just given kind of, you know, our biggest value driver is becoming a customer, so we look for companies that can provide value to our business, try to get them on board, get them on a call, potentially do a POC, and hopefully that signs a commercial.
And then we potentially look to invest.
We have a financial fund, which is basically like strategic fund is aligned in that commercial engagement with the business unit, with an investment.
But the financial fund allows us the flexibility to invest ahead of the business unit.
We say there's no guarantee that the business unit does work.
But as I mentioned, that's our biggest value driver.
So that's what we're always looking to do.
So I work with the sales and marketing teams.
We spend a lot of money, obviously, on marketing.
And so I came across your company on one of the – well, actually, a while ago, and then saw them again on, like, one of the market maps, I believe, from, like, Memo Ventures.
So just wanted to reach out again just to learn more about the company.
And really what I'll ask at the end of this is just to send a deck, pass it along to the business unit, and see if there's any interest in them having to call.
Other things I can share.
I'm a principal here.
I've here for about four years now.
Did investment bank for six and half years prior to that.
From an investment perspective, we focus on series A to C deals, but it's really A plus at this point.
Check size is, like, one to five million.
And we're pretty –
Broaden our focus.
Really, a lot of it's enterprise applications.
So AI, ML, data management analytics, cyber sales and marketing, obviously, insure tech and a bunch of variety of other verticals that we focus on.
We're a personalized property and cash to insurer.
We do have other lines.
We have a very small commercial book of business now, less of a focus now.
We have other specialty lines, such as landlords, boat, renters, and then we have other business services as well.
So a lot of areas that we can play in those services include telematics, include, what's it called, dealer services, roadside services, consumer electronic warrant, warranty and identity protection.
So that's kind of like where we play, have to answer any questions there.
Otherwise, we'd love to just, you if you have a deck that you can walk through or just an overview, we'd love to just learn more and see if this can be a potential fit for Allstate.
Yeah, absolutely, Chris.
That sounds great.
An intro on my side, I'm the founder.

Founding AE here at GrowthX, so I work hand-in-hand with our CEO, Marcel, and I was digging through the email thread and saw you reached out like back in September, so I'm sorry it took so long to get this intro call on the calendar.
As I'm sure you can imagine, like, Marcel's a pretty busy guy, and before I got here, he was the only person in charge of sales.
Andy, maybe we just get like a quick intro from you as well, and then I'll just jump right into the GrowthX rundown.
Yeah, for sure.
Nice to meet you, Tyler.
So I'm Andy, I'm an associate on the team.
I've been with the firm about six months now.
Started out my career in investment banking for a couple years after that.
I co-founded a healthcare services company, actually in Dallas.
We operated in Houston as well, and a couple other cities throughout Texas.
I exited that over the summer, and then, like I said, joined up about six months ago.
Awesome.
Chris, two quick questions before we get started.
I guess the first one is, are you always like evaluating different software startups in both life?
Is a, you know, commercial for Allstate and investment perspective or are there scenarios where it's just like commercial for the business and there's not an investment opportunity connected to it?
Because I know Marcel would be the one that would be your point of contact for anything investment related.
Yeah, I think that's why he didn't want to have the conversation I think before was because I think you guys raise or you're profitable and whatnot.

But we are, we like to talk to companies even if you're not raising or if you're raising in the future because it's like it takes time and we like to get our position, especially when it comes to marketing.
That's why I want to have a call is to really learn more.
So like if it's not even a fit, if you're not even raising, it's like we can still pass it along.
We can still be, I mean, obviously there's less of a push for us.
Obviously, we like to invest in companies or have the potential, but we're always looking to, like I said, we're the tip of the spear when it comes to innovation.
So we're always bringing new companies to the business unit and can try to facilitate and help push as much.
So we can, obviously, if there's a way to invest and we want to invest, that gives us a little bit more of incentive to push.

Otherwise, what we do here, if you're not looking to invest, it's like typically like what you say at the end of this is like get a deck, pass it along to the potential business unit, which I'm curious who exactly seems like this is kind of an overarching kind of end-to-end platform.
So curious who you sell to and then kind of see if there's interest in getting them on the call.
Okay, awesome.
No, I love that flexibility and like you never know where these things go.
And the first question to answer, right, is like, could we help out Allstate's marketing team?
The high level on GrowthX is that, you know, you can think of us as like an end-to-end partner for SEO and GEO.

So we'll do everything from identify a strategy and then actually like create and publish content or refresh content on a daily basis so that you can get more visibility in AI search so that you can show up in more answers.
Yeah.
And
You our first goal is like to drive higher intent organic traffic, but then we'll do all of the visibility and monitoring.
So we'll actually like see that through to conversions, piggybacking on your existing analytics.
With that high level, I guess help me just with the pitch, right?
Like connect it to like the problem that the marketing team would be looking to solve as it relates to content in particular.
Like where do you feel like the team needs the most help and where would they benefit the most from a partner like, you know, what we do since our offering is pretty broad?
Yeah, that's truthfully the reason why I wanted to kind of get on a call and learn a little bit more because I've seen the website and it seems like it's very, very broad.
So I was just curious kind of like where the main sticking points are, the main use cases that you're working with.
It seems like it's a managed service slash agency.
So I'm curious, it seems like the logos that I saw was more like startup type companies would make sense if you're running essentially like an outside agency for them.
It was.
Curious kind of how you're working with enterprises if you are.
That was really the reason this is just really trying to get an understanding, better understanding.
Obviously, you can only learn so much from the site or looking at like an AI engine.
So it's really just helping us to kind of get a broad overview of the product.
From a standpoint, from what you said, like AEO or GEO and SEO, I we have obviously a focus on that.
And obviously, everyone internally is trying to get a better understanding of how we're looking at with these AI engines.
And so there has been, I mean, truthfully, they're working with someone at this point.
That was something that I helped push for them just to give you an example of how we work.
So I had a bunch of calls with a bunch of different companies, and they ended up signing a contract with one of them.
So if that's kind of the main focus, it's probably less of an option, I guess, for the business unit right now.
But then we also have SEO as well, which that's both under R.
up.
So Thank
Kind of like paid search and social team.
And so they're always looking to accelerate new content.
I think the toughest part when it comes to us, to be honest, is like they have one, they're working with outside human actual writers.
So that could be helpful in understanding exactly what to write, but then also two, there's a legal review or legal component as well.
Gotcha.
Gotcha.
Yeah, on the enterprise side, Ramp is a customer we work with, Webflow, Sentinel One, Udemy, Okta.
So we definitely like, I've worked with a lot of enterprise software companies.
And we've honestly been really interested in starting to like explore partnerships outside of software because like what we do, it's really just like creating quality editorial content at scale.
But that can expand to like different work streams.
So programmatic SEO is definitely a work stream that we've done, tools and calculators.
I was looking through, like, some of the highest performing, you know, Allstate content from, like, the resources section of the site.
And I'm just going to share my screen and kind of walk you through a few things that I saw.
And so is your main, is your, are you kind of focusing on, I I know it's, you mentioned GEO and SEO.
So are you kind of combining, like, similar to, like, an Aerofs and then competing with, like, things like Profound?
Is that kind of how you're, how you're working, as well as potentially companies like, yeah, I mean, you're, you're definitely on the right track there, right?
Like, we have this platform that powers all of the work that we do.
So we're using, like, these super sophisticated AI workflows and then SEO experts.
It's the best of both worlds, right?
And we built the platform initially on Aerofs and Marcel, you know, could speak more about this, but, like, due to some limitations there, we actually just built our.
Our own platform.
And we're launching a platform that is very similar to Profound, but we also think is like addressing some gaps with that product.
And so we started off like delivering this as a service powered by an internal platform, but we're launching products very soon that the team could also use without GrowthX.
So there's some interesting ways I think in which we could engage, but we have just been like an extension of a content team to date.
You can see the SEMrush page that I'm sharing right now, correct?
Yes.
Okay, perfect.

So yeah, I mean, I think like looking through the resources section, which pages are driving the most traffic, I would say that all of these examples represent content that's very similar to like what we've created for other clients, right?
It's like really going audience first and thinking about like, what are the most important questions to answer?
And there's an art and science, right?
There's like developing the context.
That feeds into the workflows around who your personas are, what their pain points or frustrations might be.
But then there's also all the keyword analysis against your top competitors, right?
Because we want to go after like real opportunities.
And our point of view is that like SEO and GEO, the way that you win, it's not too different in terms of the way you win at each.
But there is like a structure and a format to the content that LLMs are going to favor, right, over like the traditional way of going about SEO.
And so like looking at some of this content, I think the first thing that stood out is we recommend like refreshing content every three to six months.
And so a lot of like some of the highest performing pages, there's an opportunity to like refresh some of that content, right?
And I think like these are very basic pages and we can go much deeper or much more niche beyond this.
But one benefit that teams...
Especially at the enterprise level from GrowthX is this idea of like being able to scale quality and have your content team maybe focus on more like the pillar content, right?
Like the big projects, not the thousands of pages that you need to create and refresh that approach a topic from all these different angles.

But then what's nice about what we can do is that we can build these custom workflows so that, you know, when you go beyond the editorial into, because we've done this for Ramp, like how to make ACH payments, like hundreds of different pages we've created on their blog and refreshed.

But then when you look at something like this car insurance calculator, you know, that reminds me of like the per diem calculator that we created for Ramp, which is a programmatic play where, you know, you can start off by like filling in dates, choosing a state and a county, or even like using this interactive map to dive into the specific city.

But there's also like hundreds of programmatic pages that we created for every single city so that if someone searches what's the average per diem in Houston, like Ramp has a page that answers that question.

So like whether it's, you know, something that is more programmatic like this, editorial, like the content that's driving the most traffic from the resources section, or something more custom, this is like less applicable to your industry.
But we've done like this vendor catalog where, you know, it's one of the highest performing parts of their site.
Ramp wants to be the authority in business spending.

And so now for all like the popular B2B tools, you know, what we did is we created like these interactive charts that use their internal data around spend so that now like people can start to see like, how much should I expect to spend on different tools or, you know, what's the difference between Airtable and ClickUp or Airtable and Monday?
Is this going be like an idea of the type of content that, you know, we can create and what's possible?
Any questions that are top of mind right now?
So right now you're, the focus that you guys provide is more SEO optimization, correct?
And helping to update landing pages?
Is that where I should be?
Is that kind of like the concept?
I would say SEO and GEO, with the emphasis like more on GEO right now.
So a good example there is like with Augment Code, they're in the enterprise AI coding space.
We've created like hundreds of these guides for them, and it includes like all the image generation.
If you like look into one of these examples, a lot of times there's code snippets in the content that we're creating.
So what we can do is like we can fine tune the technology to be very specific to the brand that we're working with.
We'll like ingest resources.
And then we'll start to create these artifacts where if you look at like Engine and the business travel space, you know, we've created all these business travel guides.

And if you look into some of their personas artifacts that actually feed into the workflows, we're going like super deep into who they're targeting and then having that context feed into every piece of content that we create.
And so, you know, when you see how like the content's being made, it's much different than like someone using ChatGPT or creating like a custom GPT to produce a piece of content.
Like what we're doing is, you know, we're going in and we're basically leveraging all of these different tools.
So we'll use like EXA and TAVLI for more specialized research.
We'll use a variety of different models versus like one specific model.
And then we have all these artifacts that feed into the context.

So that for the research, you know, there's like 30 minutes of research that's happening and then the output is everything that you're seeing here, which then informs like how the content gets created.
But what's nice is that the experts have the ability to make changes to any of this or even to like use the platform with those artifacts to like give commands.

So like everything that's being done, it's starting with a strategy and then it's leveraging these multi-step agentic workflows so that, you know, every part of this is thinking about how do we cross link to the right pages on the Allstate site?

How do we actually do deep fact checking so that, you know, after we've drafted something, you can actually go in and then take that entire article, extract passages and then do deep research to validate everything that's written.
And if we can't, you know, assign a certain confidence interval, what we'll do is suggest a rewrite, which our editors can then accept.
So I'm jumping around a bit just in the interest of time, but there's like a lot under the hood here of like how each step gets performed.
And so this gives you just like a quick snapshot of each step and how long it's taking to run.
But for each one of those steps, there's all these inputs.
It's informed by the strategy sprint, which is this eight-week engagement where we develop these artifacts.
We develop a roadmap and a strategy.

And then teams that are at the enterprise level, they like working with us because the end-to-end approach, the flexibility of the type of content we can create, the ability to actually, you know, do what they would do using a tool like Profound as a service, and then soon as a product, but then also, you know, it's all rooted in like for augment code.

Driving higher-intech traffic at an accelerated rate and then being able to position them with the content that we've created as the, you know, the answer to the key questions that their audience is asking.
Yeah, and just curious because we have looked at a lot of these companies, like how do you differentiate yourself?
Andy, can you hear Chris?
Your audio cut out for a second there.
Can you hear me?
Yeah, I can.
Can you hear me now or no?
Give me one second.
No.
Can you hear me now?
Yeah, my phone rang and it was connected to Bluetooth, but now we're ignored.
We have looked at a lot of these companies, so how would you, I think the biggest thing from our, from the team internally, you know, they would definitely wonder they are how you just just...

Differentiated from, say, like, the day dreams of the world, the Aerofs, Profounds, you know, we've looked at Evertoon, we've looked at Scrunch, just curious, kind of, we're seeing a lot of these companies come about, just curious how you would differentiate yourself from these and what would make an Allstate go with you over these other companies?
Yeah, super great question.
I would say the first piece is really just going to come back to, like, our origin story.
know, Marcel, he has a history of, like, building organic content engines.
IBM, he built this website, securityintelligence.com.
It was their highest ROI marketing channel, drove over 50 million in pipeline in the first year.
HP, HP, built, like, he built, contributor network of a thousand experts, delivered, like, a million monthly organic traffic, and also, you know, was, like, their highest ROI marketing channel.
He was the CMO at scale as they were acquired by Meta.
He was the marketing leader at HashiCorp from six to a hundred million.
And then at DeepGram, he thought about where can I use workflows to accelerate and to enhance the process, automate the process that can be automated.
And he just had incredible results at DeepGram, right?
So like 24X their traffic, 4X their revenue.
And so the first piece is like what we've built is coming from a proven track record of, you know, basically accomplishing what every company that buys these tools is trying to do.

And then when you think about like the AirOps versus GrowthX conversation, a lot of it comes back to, well, how successful are teams when they use a platform where everything's dependent on them to orchestrate these workflows that have so many steps that you may need to refactor as models change that may actually be more expensive when it comes.
It to all the tokens and API calls that you're making.
And then the other piece I would say when you think about like GrowthX versus Profound or GrowthX versus, you know, other agencies is the end-to-end nature.
We can do the staging and publishing.
When you think about, you know, Scrunch, Scrunch is actually a partner of ours.
So we use them for analytics, but we're also like using everything that we've learned by actually like driving traffic for clients.
Using AI and humans to create content to create products that address the gaps that we're seeing with some of these products that are already out there.
And so like, you know, if you think about Scrunch and Profound, the onus is on the user to actually identify the prompts.
And that's like one thing that we're doing with our product is like auto-generating the prompts for you.
So it's more like a hands-on approach, essentially.
Like managed service kind of approach versus like self
That's a good way to think about it.
Yeah.
Extension of your team, but like, there's also a technology element.
And so other agencies probably don't have that technology element that the customers can use.
Cool.
I mean, it's super helpful.
I would like to, if you have a marketing deck that I can share, I will pass it along to the physician.
I do wish we had a conversation a few months ago because they, like I mentioned, they have signed a contract with someone.
So they're not obviously looking right now, but, you that could obviously, you know, depending on results and kind of when they work with this company, that could change in the coming months.
So I think it's definitely kind of a slight no right now, but maybe potentially an opportunity in the future.
But I would like to have the decks that way I can share with them because basically, as I mentioned, we do, as we come across new companies like yourself, we do pass that along.
So that way, if they are looking for a new partner, they can.
Thank you, buddy, you guys.
Yeah, 100%.
In SEO or GEO, AEO side, we could be beneficial in either direction.
And I think the thing that would be helpful in the deck to share is, you know, we start off with the strategy sprint where you have the ability to, like, opt in to the 12-month execution.
So you can really kind of see what we're capable of in that eight-week sprint if they wanted to explore us, like, one of their partners.
But did you or Andy have any other questions that I can help answer today?
No, I think this is great.
Yeah, I just think it's good to learn about the company.
It's just unfortunate because we were digging in probably since, like, April of, like, year.
So it's unfortunate, at at this point.
But obviously, you know, when they come up, I mean, it's not a long-term contract, you know, multi-year.
So not like a multi-year or so as they come up and potentially assess, you know.
You know, obviously, I'll keep you guys in the loop.
Yeah, that's good, Chris.
And the one thing that I think is good on the timing side is that we will be, like, launching these products over the next, you know, two to three months.
And so if it would be helpful to do an intro once our products are out there, you know, let me know.
I'd be happy to at least give the team the rundown.
I'm curious, when you say launching products, are there certain, from what you described, are there certain products that have not launched yet?
Is there certain ones that have?
I'm just curious, kind of, where that is, just so I'm aware of, you know, when I send the deck and if they are interested.
Yeah, for sure.
And so I would say, like, what you're seeing here, it's all internal, but that will be, like, public-facing soon.
And this is more of, like, an end-to-end, right, like, using the workflows to create content.
So instead of building from scratch, being able to, like, leverage workflows that already exist, customize them.
And then what's, I would say, closer, like, within the next month to launching.
Is the tool.

Well, that's going to be more like profound and, you know, we think like better in the sense of being able to monitor and understand where you're showing up, what to create, and where to focus the time and energy when it comes to AEO.
Okay, cool.
Awesome.
Thank you.
I appreciate it.
Yeah, you could send that deck.
That'd be helpful.
And I'll keep you posted on interest and as they come up, as I get feedback on what they're working with.
Yeah, sounds great.
Well, good to start the conversation and meet you both.
And yeah, keep me in the loop as you hear more from the team.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Have a good one.
Thanks, Tyler.
You too.
See ya.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce GrowthX's SEO/AEO services to Allstate Ventures.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**GrowthX offers a managed service** combining AI workflows with human experts to scale high-quality SEO/AEO content, freeing internal teams for pillar projects.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=638.0)
  - [**The immediate opportunity is limited:** Allstate's marketing team recently signed a contract with a competitor, making them unavailable for new partners.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [**A future opportunity exists:** The marketing team's contract is short-term, and GrowthX is launching two new products (a Profound-like tool and an end-to-end platform) that could be a better fit later.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1402.0)
  - [**Next step:** Tyler will send a deck for Chris to share with the marketing team to keep GrowthX on their radar for future needs.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)

## Topics

### Allstate Ventures' Role & Investment Focus

  - [**Function:** Allstate Ventures (ASV) is the "tip of the spear" for innovation, sourcing startups for Allstate's business units.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=90.0)
  - [**Primary Goal:** Drive commercial engagement (POC → contract) before considering investment.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
  - [**Funds:**](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [**Strategic Fund:** Aligns investment with a commercial deal.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [**Financial Fund:** Allows investment ahead of a commercial deal.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=122.0)
  - [**Investment Criteria:**](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
      - [**Stage:** Series A+](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
      - [**Check Size:** $1M–$5M](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
      - [**Focus:** Enterprise applications (AI/ML, sales/marketing, Insurtech).](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)

### GrowthX's Offering & Differentiation

  - [**Core Service:** An end-to-end managed service for SEO and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization).](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1108.0)
    1.  [**Strategy Sprint:** An 8-week engagement to develop a content roadmap and "artifacts" (personas, pain points) that guide all content creation.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1108.0)
    2.  [**Execution:** AI-powered workflows (using tools like EXA, Tavli) generate content, which human experts then review, refine, and fact-check.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
  - [**Differentiation:**](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1157.0)
      - [**Managed Service:** Provides a "hands-on" extension of the client's team, unlike self-serve tools (e.g., Profound, Scrunch) that require users to build prompts and workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1359.0)
      - [**Proven Track Record:** Founder Marcel's background includes building high-ROI content engines for IBM, HP, and DeepGram (24x traffic, 4x revenue).](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)
      - [**Proprietary Tech:** GrowthX built its own platform after finding limitations in tools like Ahrefs.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=650.0)

### Allstate's Need & Current Status

  - [**Identified Need:** The marketing team struggles to scale content creation due to reliance on human writers and a slow legal review process.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=542.0)
  - [**Current Status:** The team recently signed a contract with a competitor for AEO services.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [**Future Opportunity:** The contract is short-term, creating a potential opening for GrowthX in the coming months.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1402.0)

### Upcoming Product Launches

  - [**Profound-like Tool:** Launching within one month.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1505.0)
      - [**Function:** Monitor AEO performance and identify content opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1554.0)
      - [**Key Differentiator:** Auto-generates prompts, reducing user effort.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1359.0)
  - [**End-to-End Platform:** Launching in 2–3 months.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1505.0)
      - [**Function:** Public-facing version of GrowthX's internal workflow platform.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1521.0)
      - [**Benefit:** Allows clients to leverage pre-built, customizable workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1521.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send the GrowthX marketing deck to Chris.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)
  - [**Chris:** Share the deck with the Allstate marketing team to keep GrowthX on their radar.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)
  - [**Chris:** Inform Tyler of any future opportunities or feedback from the marketing team.](https://fathom.video/share/rwv3xNAo_tVNMXwD_w8-8uXBGYxovLrr?tab=summary&timestamp=1583.0)

action_items.description: Email Chris marketing deck incl. 8-wk strategy sprint + 12-mo execution
